title: Investigate cross-session dome-sync-clone cleanup — the /sessions/*/dome-sync-clone sweep added 2026-05-29 is in FUSE but apparently not catching orphans (operator cleaned 3 stale clones this morning, all 10-15h old, all post-fix-deployment)
background:
Yesterday (2026-05-29T15:20Z, commit 58c73c5) the operator extended workspace-sync.md's stale-clone sweep to cover /sessions/*/dome-sync-clone in addition to /tmp/*. The fix was bootstrapped to FUSE 2026-05-30T05:11Z (commit 9203f8c) via PROP-064's manual sync-workspace. Verification at the time: the sweep was confirmed present in FUSE and 0 orphans existed.

This morning (2026-05-31T~05:00Z) the operator found and manually cleaned 3 stale dome-sync-clone directories under /sessions/. Ages at cleanup time:
  - 10 hours old (newest)
  - ~12 hours old (middle)
  - 15 hours old (oldest)

All three are POST the fix being in FUSE (≥17h after the bootstrap). So workspace-sync's hourly cycles SHOULD have swept them — the find command has `-mmin +60` (older than 60 minutes) so anything ≥1h stale qualifies.

The sweep IS in the FUSE prompt (operator verified). But it's not removing these clones. Possible causes:
  (a) **Permissions**: /sessions/<other-id>/ directories are owned by `nobody:nogroup` with mode 750 (drwxr-x---). The workspace-sync agent runs as a per-session user (the cowork sandbox user, distinct from `nobody`). That user is not in `nogroup`, so they have NO read/execute access into other sessions' directories. The find command's `2>/dev/null` swallows permission-denied errors silently. Net: workspace-sync's sweep can never SEE clones in other sessions, much less delete them.
  (b) **The sweep IS finding them but the rm -rf is failing silently** (same swallowed-error pattern). Less likely given mode 750 — even reading the directory listing would fail.
  (c) **The sweep ran but matched 0 paths because at sweep-time the clones were < 60min old** — possible if a recent workspace-sync invocation just created them. But the user found 3 clones 10-15h old; that's many sweep cycles past the threshold.
  (d) **workspace-sync isn't actually running Step 1's sweep block** because of the same path issue investigated in DIRECTIVE-20260531-002 (Step 4c silent skip). Less likely — Step 1 is imperative and other code in it (the disk check, the trap, the pull) clearly runs.

Operator hypothesis (a) is the most likely. If correct, the sweep can NEVER work cross-session via the current mechanism.

evidence.operator_cleanup_this_morning: 3 stale dome-sync-clone directories under /sessions/, ages 10h / 12h / 15h. Free space recovered: roughly 3 × ~90MB = ~270MB.
evidence.fix_was_in_FUSE: Operator verified 2026-05-30T05:11Z that monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md in FUSE contained the `find /sessions -maxdepth 2 -type d -name 'dome-sync-clone' -mmin +60` line. Verification command: `grep -A 2 'find /sessions' /sessions/intelligent-brave-lamport/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md`.
evidence.perms_observation: ls -la /sessions/ shows directories owned by nobody:nogroup with mode drwxr-x--- (750). Other-user access is rwx for nobody, r-x for nogroup, nothing for others. Workspace-sync's cowork-Haiku user is not in nogroup.
evidence.operator_cleanup_works_because: Operator's shell has different access perms than the cowork-Haiku agent (likely owner-equivalent or root-equivalent for /sessions/ via the cowork sandbox's host mount). That's why operator can clean what workspace-sync can't see.

requested_work: Step 1 — Confirm operator hypothesis (a): from a workspace-sync run's bash session, attempt `ls /sessions/<some-other-id>/dome-sync-clone 2>&1` and report the exit code + stderr. If 'Permission denied' or 'No such file or directory' (after the directory genuinely exists per operator-side ls), hypothesis (a) is confirmed.

Step 2 — Design a cross-session cleanup that DOESN'T require workspace-sync to see other sessions:
  (i) **Session-self-cleanup via better EXIT trap**: each session's workspace-sync should clean ITS OWN clone reliably on exit. The current EXIT trap (line 128 of workspace-sync.md) is supposed to do this. If clones survive a session's end, the trap isn't firing reliably (LLM bash sessions don't always trigger EXIT). Add a post-Step-5 explicit `rm -rf $CLONE` (which already exists in the Cleanup section) — confirm this fires. If it doesn't, investigate why.
  (ii) **Use /tmp instead of /sessions for the clone**: $CLONE = /tmp/dome-sync-clone — then the existing /tmp/* sweep covers it. Cleaner. But operator chose /sessions for sandbox-isolation reasons; need to evaluate whether that constraint still holds.
  (iii) **Operator-side cron/scheduled cleanup**: a separate scheduled task (similar to dome-prune-integrity) that runs as a user with cross-session read perms and sweeps /sessions/*/dome-sync-clone. New agent, new scheduled task. Heavy-handed but reliable.
  (iv) **Accept operator does periodic manual cleanup**: status quo. Operator-side cost: ~30 sec per cleanup × every few days. Probably 1-2 min/week.

Step 3 — Recommend ONE option, author PROP-066 (or amend PROP-051 or PROP-064). If recommendation is (ii) — moving CLONE back to /tmp — include a side-by-side comparison of sandbox-isolation concerns vs. the current cross-session-orphan accumulation. If recommendation is (i), enumerate why the existing EXIT trap may not be firing and design a more reliable cleanup pattern.
Step 4 — If recommendation isn't (iv), include the file ownership / build.js OWNERSHIP impact (if any) and any CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules touchpoints.
non_goals: Do NOT add a system-level cron job or root-equivalent process — out of scope for the operator's cowork environment.
Do NOT change the per-session clone NAME or its workspace-sync semantics — just the cleanup path.

context_pointers.the_fuse_bootstrap: 9203f8c at 2026-05-30T05:11Z — PROP-064 commit, which manually sync-workspace'd the fix into FUSE
context_pointers.operator_clean_evidence: this directive's filed_at minus 10-15h = 2026-05-30T14:00-19:00Z window for clone creation; operator cleanup at 2026-05-31T~05:00Z
context_pointers.workspace_sync_step_1: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md ~line 64 (the find command) + the EXIT trap at ~line 128 + the final Cleanup section at ~line 1197

expected_output: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-066.json (or PROP-051/064 amendment). Diagnostic confirmation in Step 1 + recommendation + patch.
estimated_effort: Mode 1 diagnostic + Mode 4 PROP-authoring. ~20-30 min Opus.
priority_note: MEDIUM — not a data-loss event, just an operator-side ~30s cleanup every couple of days. The TRUE concern is that this exposes the cowork-Haiku agent's perms limitations; future operator-direct fixes that assume the agent can sweep other sessions will fail the same way. The diagnostic value of confirming hypothesis (a) is higher than the cleanup-savings value.
